message:
v0.3.10: Directional calibration guard and improved executor diagnostics

Version bump 0.3.8 → 0.3.10.

guardrails.rs — Directional rate error (major behavioral change):
- Replace symmetric rate_error_ratio() with directional rate_danger_ratio()
  that only penalizes UNDERESTIMATION of disk consumption. Overestimation
  (EWMA predicts more filling than reality) is the safe direction for a
  cleanup system — it triggers earlier cleanup, never late.
- This fixes production failures where post-burst EWMA decay (predicted >>
  actual while reality returns to baseline) permanently poisoned the
  calibration guard. The EWMA is correctly recovering during this phase;
  its overestimation is safe, not miscalibration.
- Exclude burst_outlier observations from calibration_metrics() entirely.
  During bursts, rate error and TTE divergence are expected (EWMA damps
  the spike). Including them permanently skewed the calibration window
  on bursty machines (600+ consecutive breach windows observed on
  production servers running rustc compilations).
- Return neutral metrics (0.0, 1.0) when all observations are burst
  outliers instead of (INFINITY, 0.0) which would immediately trip FAIL.
- Make e_process_reward symmetric with penalty in log-space:
  reward = 2/3 ≈ 1/1.5 so ln(reward) = -ln(penalty). This ensures
  good observations recover as fast as bad ones accumulate, preventing
  permanent drift alarm on bursty workloads.
- Add comprehensive tests: post-burst EWMA decay scenario,
  underestimation vs overestimation, symmetric e_process recovery,
  rename existing tests to match new naming.

loop_main.rs — Executor diagnostics:
- Enrich periodic summary report with full guard diagnostic fields
  (e_process value, median rate error, conservative fraction, observation
  count, consecutive clean windows) instead of bare pass/fail/unknown.
- Add log lines when all approved candidates are dampened (helps diagnose
  why executor produces no deletions during pressure events).
- Add log line when plan() filters all approved candidates (shows
  min_score threshold and dry_run state for debugging).

policy.rs — Test fix:
- Update drift_alarm_triggers_fallback test to use Orange pressure
  (was Yellow), matching the v0.3.8+ behavior where guard drift only
  triggers fallback at Orange+ pressure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
 [package]
 name = "storage_ballast_helper"
-version = "0.3.9"
+version = "0.3.10"
 edition = "2024"
 license = "MIT"
 description = "System service that prevents disk-full scenarios from coding agent swarms via ballast files, artifact scanning, and special location monitoring."
